title: python3/fucking_coffee.py passes str to telnetlib instead of bytes
body: conn.write(COFFEE_MACHINE_PASS) and conn.write('sys brew') pass plain str objects, but Python 3's telnetlib.Telnet.write() requires a bytes-like object. Running this under python3 raises TypeError instead of brewing coffee.

title: smack-my-bitch-up.sh has no Windows/PowerShell equivalent
body: Every other prank script in the repo has ports across several languages, but smack-my-bitch-up.sh only exists as a POSIX shell script relying on 'who' and Twilio curl calls. Could we get a PowerShell version for parity like the other scripts have across languages?

title: Coffee machine IP and password are hardcoded instead of read from .env
body: hackerutils.py already has a get_dotenv() helper that loads a .env file, but python3/fucking_coffee.py still hardcodes COFFEE_MACHINE_ADDR and COFFEE_MACHINE_PASS at the top of the file instead of pulling them from the dotenv helper like the rest of the scripts seem to intend.

title: hangover.sh and fucking-coffee.sh silently succeed even when curl fails to reach Twilio
body: Both hangover.sh and fucking-coffee.sh check $? after the curl call, but curl's -f flag combined with backtick command substitution means a network failure can still leave $? as 0 in some shells, so the 'Failed to send SMS' branch never triggers when it should.

title: go/fucking-coffee.go and python3/fucking_coffee.py disagree on the brew delay
body: The Go version sleeps 17s (delayBeforeBrew) then waits 24s (delay) after 'sys brew' before sending 'sys pour', matching the Python version's timings, but the Go version also adds a 10-minute timeout per Expect call that has no equivalent safeguard in the Python version, which can hang forever if the coffee machine doesn't respond.
